where applicable, and all applicable state and local nondiscrimination requirements. The program does not discriminate
Admission, discharge, discipline, access to services, and program participation decisions are based on legitimate safety,
program suitability, documented conduct, and the ability of the program to provide a safe recovery residence
environment. Reasonable accommodation requests will be reviewed promptly and individually. The program will not
impose blanket exclusions where individualized assessment is required.

Confidential Operational Document | Page 4SECTION 1: ORGANIZATIONAL DOCUMENTATION
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center exists to break the cycle of homelessness and empower underserved
individuals through structured recovery housing, accountability, peer support, and access to community-based supportive
services that promote long-term stability, sobriety, and independent living.
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center envisions a community where every individual, regardless of background or
circumstance, has access to safe housing, meaningful recovery support, and the opportunity to achieve sustainable,
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center provides recovery-oriented supportive housing in a shared living environment.
The program is not a hospital, detoxification facility, treatment center, medical clinic, or licensed clinical provider. The
program provides safe housing, structure, accountability, peer support, recovery expectations, community referrals, and
documentation systems that support stability and recovery.
The residence operates as a substance-free recovery residence. Residents are expected to participate in recovery-

Confidential Operational Document | Page 8SECTION 3: CORE POLICIES AND PROCEDURES
Protect resident privacy and ensure records are secured.
Resident information is confidential. Information may not be disclosed unless the resident signs written consent,
disclosure is required by court order, a medical emergency requires limited disclosure, mandatory reporting applies, or
there is a credible threat of harm that requires action.
All physical records must be stored in a locked file cabinet or locked office. Digital records must be password protected.
Staff may only access records needed for their assigned role. Resident information shall not be discussed in public areas,
with other residents, on social media, or with family members without written consent. Any suspected breach must be
Consent to Release Information; Resident File Checklist; Confidentiality Acknowledgment; Incident Report for breach.
Ensure disclosures are authorized and limited.
The program will use written consent before sharing resident information unless a legal or emergency exception applies.
Consent must identify who may receive information, what information may be shared, the purpose, expiration, and
Staff must verify that a valid release exists before speaking with outside parties. Residents may revoke consent in writing
unless action has already been taken based on the consent. Releases are stored in the resident file.
Consent to Release Information Form; Revocation Note if applicable.
and orientation scheduling. If the applicant appears to need medical detox, emergency care, or a higher level of care,

Confidential Operational Document | Page 6SECTION 2: CODE OF ETHICS AND PROFESSIONAL STANDARDS
To establish ethical standards for staff, volunteers, peer leaders, and leadership.
All representatives of Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center must act with integrity, honesty, fairness, accountability,
and respect for resident dignity. Staff shall protect resident welfare, avoid exploitation, maintain confidentiality, report
safety concerns, and follow program policies.
Staff must review this Code of Ethics during onboarding and annually thereafter. Any staff member who observes
unethical conduct must report it to the Executive Director. Ethics concerns involving the Executive Director may be
reported to the Board or governing authority designated by the organization.
To protect residents from exploitation and maintain professional house operations.
Staff, volunteers, and peer leaders shall not enter into romantic, sexual, financial, lending, borrowing, employment, or
private business relationships with current residents. Staff shall not accept gifts of significant value, control resident
money, or use resident labor for personal benefit.
Boundary violations must be reported immediately and documented as an incident. Corrective action may include
removal from schedule, retraining, discipline, or termination of service.
To ensure residents understand their protections and expectations.
Residents have the right to dignity, safety, fair treatment, confidentiality, grievance access, reasonable accommodation
policy, emergency exits, evacuation location, Good Neighbor expectations, chore assignments, fees, phase expectations,
work for the program as a condition of housing unless the work is ordinary shared household chores. Paid work, if
offered, must be voluntary, documented, paid as agreed, and not used to avoid employment laws.
Staff provide written fee expectations during intake. Hardship requests are reviewed by the Executive Director. Any paid
task must be approved in writing before work begins.
Fee Acknowledgment; Hardship Request; Paid Work Authorization if used.
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center | Policy & Procedures Manual
Residents are subject to intake, random, reasonable suspicion, post-incident, and return-to-house drug and alcohol
testing. Refusal, tampering, adulteration, or failure to appear for a scheduled test is treated as a positive result for
Testing must be conducted respectfully and consistently. Results are documented on the Drug Testing Log. Positive tests
trigger the Recurrence of Use and Restoration Policy. Staff must avoid shaming language and document facts only.
Drug Testing Attestation; Drug Testing Log; Incident Report for positive/refusal.
3.8 Recurrence of Use and Restoration Policy
Respond to substance use with safety, accountability, and recovery support.
A recurrence of use is treated as a safety and recovery event, not a moral failure. The program may require temporary
removal, increased testing, recovery meetings, referral to higher care, restoration contract, or discharge based on
Staff document the event, conduct immediate safety assessment, determine if emergency help is needed, notify approved
emergency contact only if release permits or emergency exists, and review next steps with the Executive Director.
Restoration may include increased meetings, sponsor contact, testing 1 to 2 times per week for 30 days, and written
Incident Report; Recurrence Assessment; Restoration Contract; Drug Testing Log.
Support safe self-management of prescribed medications while recognizing program limits.
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center does not prescribe, administer, or manage medication as a medical provider.
Residents are responsible for taking their own medication as prescribed. Medications must not be shared, sold, traded,
left unsecured, or used contrary to prescription instructions. Medication-assisted treatment and other prescribed
medications are reviewed through an individualized safety process and are not automatically prohibited.
Residents disclose prescribed medications at intake and update staff when prescriptions change. Staff may require
medications to be secured in a locked location if safety concerns exist. Staff may document medication counts only when
part of a written safety plan. Alcohol-containing products and over-the-counter products that threaten sobriety may be
restricted when clinically or safety justified.
Medication Record; Medication Safety Plan if needed; Incident Report for misuse.
Protect residents from weapons, drugs, alcohol, and other hazards.
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center | Policy & Procedures Manual
The program may inspect rooms, common areas, personal storage, bags, packages, and vehicles on property when there
is reasonable safety concern, scheduled inspection, suspected rule violation, or documented program need. Searches
must be respectful and conducted by staff or leadership.
When possible, two staff/leadership persons should be present. Staff must document reason, items found, action taken,
and resident response. Illegal drugs or weapons must be handled according to safety and law enforcement procedures.
Search Log; Incident Report; Property Disposal Record.
Ensure all significant events are documented, reviewed, and corrected.
Incidents must be documented within 24 hours and reviewed by the Executive Director within 48 hours when possible.
Reportable incidents include overdose, medical emergency, injury, police contact, threats, violence, suspected abuse,
property damage, recurrence of use, contraband, medication misuse, resident disappearance, confidentiality breach,
grievance escalation, fire, evacuation, or neighborhood complaint. Staff document facts, witnesses, actions, notifications,
Incident Report; Incident Log; Corrective Action Plan.
Provide a fair, accessible complaint process without retaliation.
Residents may submit written or verbal grievances. Staff must assist residents who need help writing a grievance.

3.16 Emergency, EMS, and Overdose Response Policy
Provide immediate response to emergencies and overdose risk.
Call 911 for medical emergencies, overdose, fire, violence, serious injury, or immediate danger. Staff may share limited
information needed by emergency responders. Naloxone should be available where legally permitted and staff should be
trained in overdose recognition and response.
In emergency: call 911, move residents to safety, administer naloxone if overdose suspected and trained/available, do not
leave affected person alone, provide full name and relevant information to EMS, notify Executive Director, document
incident. Evacuation maps must be posted and residents must know gathering location.
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center | Policy & Procedures Manual
Emergency Contact Form; Incident Report; Overdose Response Log; Drill Log.

Ensure legal and safety obligations are met.
Confidentiality does not prevent mandated reports of abuse, neglect, exploitation, credible threats of harm, or other legally
Staff must immediately notify the Executive Director of suspected reportable concerns. Emergency threats require
immediate 911 call. Reports must be factual and documented.
Incident Report; Mandatory Reporting Note; External Report Confirmation if available.

Confidential Operational Document | Page 14SECTION 4: HEALTH, SAFETY, AND PROPERTY OPERATIONS
The residence must be clean, sanitary, structurally safe, and free of hazards. Smoke alarms, fire extinguishers, visible
address numbers, posted evacuation maps, first aid kit, clear exits, working HVAC, functional bathrooms and kitchen, and
safe electrical use are required. Five residents maximum per properly operating bathroom is the operating target unless
applicable code requires a stricter standard.
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
Fire evacuation maps must be posted in visible locations. Residents are oriented to exits and gathering location. Fire
extinguishers must be tagged and current. Smoke alarms must be tested monthly and documented. Open flames, unsafe
extension cords, disabled alarms, blocked exits, and smoking inside are prohibited.
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
The House Manager or designee completes daily checks for kitchen, bathrooms, common areas, trash, lights, doors,
hazards, exterior debris, and resident compliance. Issues are documented and corrected promptly.
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
A full monthly inspection must verify smoke alarms, fire extinguishers, posted emergency information, HVAC, appliances,
interior/exterior hazards, cleanliness, and repair needs. Monthly report is submitted to the Executive Director by the first
business day of the following month.
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center | Policy & Procedures Manual
Confidential Operational Document | Page 154.5 Maintenance and Repair
Residents and staff must report maintenance concerns immediately. Maintenance requests are logged, prioritized,
assigned, and closed when resolved. Safety hazards receive urgent attention. Damage caused by resident conduct may
result in corrective action and financial responsibility consistent with the resident agreement.
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
Illegal substances found on property are not handled casually. Staff must contact appropriate authorities when needed.
Unused prescription medication may be disposed of through an authorized medication take-back location when lawful
and with proper documentation. Staff shall not retain resident medication after discharge unless required for safety
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
Residents and staff maintain clean common areas, bathrooms, kitchen, laundry areas, and bedrooms. Shared surfaces
are cleaned routinely. Residents must report pests, bodily fluid spills, illness concerns, or sanitation hazards immediately.
Staff may require temporary enhanced cleaning during illness outbreaks.
Incident Report if injury, fire, hazard, or emergency occurs
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center | Policy & Procedures Manual

At least 21 years old for House Manager or Assistant House Manager roles.

All staff, volunteers, and peer leaders with resident access must complete background screening before unsupervised

Confidential Operational Document | Page 42SECTION 9: INTERNAL COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
The Executive Director shall document major operational changes within 48 hours of awareness. Major changes include
relocation, capacity changes, change in House Manager, serious safety concerns, ownership or governance updates, or
changes affecting resident services. Documentation shall be maintained in the internal compliance file.
Focus Rehabilitation and Ministry Center shall maintain liability insurance and any other coverage appropriate for the
residence and organizational operations. Proof of coverage shall be reviewed annually and kept in the administrative file.
Naloxone (Narcan) shall be maintained on-site when available and legally permitted. Staff and residents shall be oriented
to emergency response procedures, including when to call 911, where emergency resources are located, and how
The organization may conduct scheduled or unscheduled internal reviews to verify that the home remains clean, safe,
and policy compliant. Any outside review or inspection shall be documented and any correction plan shall be assigned,
9.5 Financial Records and Resident Fee Transparency
Resident financial agreements, program fee acknowledgments, deposits, payments, hardship approvals, and refunds
shall be documented clearly. Residents must be informed of fees and expectations before move-in and before signing
All marketing, referral, website, social media, and public representations must be truthful, verifiable, and consistent with
actual program services. The program shall not represent itself as a medical, detoxification, or clinical treatment provider
unless properly licensed to do so.
